extraction.one_line_summary: VAN DER POEL survived a 205km solo breakaway by 10 seconds after POGAČAR attacked late and closed a 245-second gap to within striking distance
tactical_patterns: Pogačar attacked on the muri finale when Van der Poel was visibly cracking, nearly bridging 4+ minutes in final kilometers alone
UAE isolated Van der Poel by exhausting him through sustained chase rather than early counterattack on coastal roads
Formolo's pacing work for UAE proved critical in setting up Pogačar's final acceleration on technical bumpy finishing circuit
Van der Poel's GC threat forced Pogačar to chase personally rather than delegate, revealing UAE's lack of stage-race climbing depth

rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: negative: admitted being 'completely empty' and unable to process radio information in final kilometers despite winning
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: closed 245 seconds to 10 seconds in finale while Van der Poel cracked, showing superior diesel engine on technical punchy walls
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: negative: dropped from contention to lose 1:15 to Pogačar on relatively short punchy climbs, signaling poor form
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: moved to third overall by gaining time on Bernal on punchy terrain, suggesting sharpness for similar profiles
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: neutral: maintained second at 1:15 but couldn't follow Pogačar's acceleration, establishing ceiling for explosive climbing
